what_it_does: A read-only deep-mine document characterizing the backend HTTP integration seams (routes + suite.py methods) of the Company application as of commit-state 2026-06-08. For each seam it states the suite.py/bridge.py file:line location, the route, the input/output contract, events emitted, guarantees, and a BUILT/DESIGNED status, with a headline correcting the parent task's assumption that several seams were net-new (proving 4 of 5 are already built).
observations: Opens with a HEADLINE that overturns the task's premise: I2 /api/act, I4 tier-gate, R1 backend-held locus, and L1 /api/intent-at are already BUILT+REACHABLE; only the standalone GET /api/context?address= route and an R2-knob setter route are genuinely DESIGNED-NOT-BUILT.
Documents the address grammar from contracts/address.py: run://, cas://, blob://, vec://, ui://, code:// schemes, and the scheme(addr) fail-loud gate.

Details the focus->locus->context-at-address channel with exact suite.py line numbers (2012, 2031, 2061, 2701, 2892) showing how focus.selected sets self._current_locus (last-wins) and R2 consumes current_locus().
Gives the verified R2 scoring formula (suite.py:2411-2443): recency=exp(-LAMBDA*age), proximity tree-distance, semantic cosine, pin_bonus, capped to R2_BUDGET.
Provides a 15-row built-seam contract table (method, route, inputs, outputs, event emitted, surface need) and a net-new status table.

Closes with 5 key findings for Claude Design: focus->locus is load-bearing, R2 has no standalone GET, /api/act is the clean click seam, every addressed write emits an addressed SSE event, /api/address-help is the single what-can-I-do-here endpoint.
standouts: The headline correction: the document's primary signal is that the task framed already-built seams as net-new, with file:line proof for each.
The two identified real gaps: GET /api/context?address= (call R2 without a chat turn) and an R2-knob setter (composition_config is read-only).
The fully-traced focus->locus->context pipeline with line numbers and the R2 scoring formula as code.
conventions: Uses an explicit status legend (BUILT+REACHABLE / BUILT-NO-FE / DESIGNED-NOT-BUILT) consistently.
Cites file:line for every claim (suite.py:XXXX, bridge.py:XXXX), matching the repo's evidence-grounded discipline.
Describes fail-loud gates (S0 grammar gate raising on malformed ui://, empty-scope DENY-ALL) consistent with the repo's Rule 4 and Rule 8.
notes: If briefing someone: this is the authoritative seam-by-seam contract sheet for the address/RHM backend, and its main message is that the surface work is mostly FE-wiring of existing routes, not new backend building.
questions: The commit-state is pinned to 2026-06-08; are the cited line numbers still valid against current main?
Is the GET /api/context?address= gap still open, or was it closed in a later session?
purpose_vs_actual: Match. Stated as a deep mine of backend seams that the capability-map was thin on, and it delivers per-seam contracts with line-level proof plus a corrected build-status picture.
apparent_themes: Address-keyed everything (every element, event, comment, chat, context resolves at a ui:// address)
Fail-loud governance (S0 gates, DENY-ALL on empty scope, tier-based dispatch)
Reflect-never-own FE (surface re-renders from the SSE stream at the address an event carries)
intention_signals: Trying to give the Claude Design effort an accurate, proof-backed map of which integration seams already exist so it wires the front-end to them rather than redesigning or rebuilding backend capability that is already shipped.
novelty: The focus->locus->context channel traced as a single backend-held-locus mechanism (R1 sets, R2 consumes) and the explicit identification that the task's net-new list was mostly wrong.
contribution: Contributes a verified, route-level contract catalog of the address/RHM backend seams (inputs, outputs, events, status, line numbers) plus the precise R2 scoring formula and the two true backend gaps; a fused system keeps this as the integration-seam reference for any surface that talks to the suite.
summary_for_embedding: A 2026-06-08 read-only deep-mine of the Company backend HTTP seams (chat, act, annotate, address_help, resolve_scope, R2 context, SSE stream) giving per-seam route/input/output/event contracts with suite.py and bridge.py line numbers and BUILT-vs-DESIGNED status.
intention_for_embedding: To give the design effort a proof-backed map of which address/RHM integration seams are already built so it wires the front-end to existing routes instead of rebuilding backend capability.
